title: Weekend in Berlin - Quick Summary
items: **Plan around your real arrival, not the hotel booking.** A Friday 18:00 arrival leaves room for Hackescher Markt and the Spree; after 21:00, eat near the hotel and protect Saturday morning.
**Make Saturday one central line.** Start at the World Clock on Alexanderplatz, walk the historic centre, choose one Museum Island building, then continue along Unter den Linden to Brandenburg Gate.

**BerlinWalk gives Saturday a clear anchor.** The Saturday tour starts at the World Clock at 11:30 and 15:30, lasts about 2 hours and ends near Hackescher Markt. It explores the historic centre and does not follow the Berlin Wall line.
**Save normal shopping for Friday or Saturday.** Most Berlin shops close on Sundays, apart from limited exceptions such as station supermarkets and officially authorised shopping dates.
**Use Sunday for Bernauer Straße and Mauerpark.** Start at Nordbahnhof, read the preserved border landscape, then continue to the Sunday flea market and weather-dependent afternoon karaoke.

**Keep a real departure buffer.** An early Sunday train or flight means leaving Mauerpark before the afternoon develops. A later departure makes Prenzlauer Berg lunch or karaoke possible without rushing.
